pattern: A straight cut gives two prefix/suffix blocks; allowing one removable cell turns the test into 'does the heavier block hold a discardable cell whose value equals the gap', where discardability depends on the block's shape.
hints: For each horizontal or vertical cut, one side is a rectangular block of leading rows or columns; the gap to close is the absolute difference of the two side sums.
Only the heavier side can shrink, and only by removing exactly one cell whose value equals that gap, while the rest must stay connected.
A solid rectangle stays connected after deleting any single cell unless it is one row or one column tall, in which case only the two end cells may be removed.

edge_inputs.description: balanced by discounting one cell in a column
discount would disconnect a single row
single column, remove an end cell
interior removal of a single column disconnects
two-by-two block allows any cell removal

stress.description: small random grids exercising both balanced and discount paths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    side = max(2, min(size, 12))
    m = rng.randint(1, side)
    n = rng.randint(1, side)
    if m * n < 2:
        n = 2
    grid = [[rng.randint(1, 20) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(m)]
    return [grid]
